commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

results.error:
OpenAI raw iterative: no compiled code produced (1 turns, 42176 tokens)
No tool calls made — model responded with text only

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout

- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

generation.sourceCode:
import React from 'react';
import { Card, Stack, Text } from '@ggui-ai/design/primitives';

interface Props {
  /** City name */
  city: string;
  /** Current temperature */
  temperature: number;
  /** Weather condition (Sunny, Cloudy, Rainy, etc.) */
  condition: string;
  /** Humidity percentage */
  humidity: number;
  /** Wind speed */
  windSpeed: number;
  /** Temperature unit (C or F) */
  unit?: string;
  /** 5-day forecast array */
  forecast: Array<{ day?: string; icon?: string; high?: number; low?: number }>;
}

export default function GeneratedComponent({
  city = 'City',
  temperature = 0,
  condition = 'Unknown',
  humidity = 0,
  windSpeed = 0,
  unit = 'C',
  forecast = [],
}: Props) {
  const resolvedUnit = unit || 'C';

  return (
    <Card
      shadow="md"
      radius="lg"
      padding="var(--ggui-spacing-6, 24px)"
      border
    >
      <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)">
        <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
          <Text
            variant="label"
            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
            as="div"
          >
            {city}
          </Text>
          <Text
            size="4xl"
            weight="bold"
            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
            as="div"
          >
            {temperature}°{resolvedUnit}
          </Text>
          <Text
            variant="bodyLarge"
            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
            as="div"
            aria-label={`Current condition: ${condition}`}
          >
            {condition}
          </Text>
        </Stack>

        <Stack
          direction="horizontal"
          justify="between"
          gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)"
          wrap
        >
          <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
            <Text
              variant="caption"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
              as="div"
            >
              Humidity
            </Text>
            <Text
              variant="body"
              weight="semibold"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
              as="div"
            >
              {humidity}%
            </Text>
          </Stack>

          <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
            <Text
              variant="caption"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
              as="div"
            >
              Wind
            </Text>
            <Text
              variant="body"
              weight="semibold"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
              as="div"
            >
              {windSpeed}
            </Text>
          </Stack>
        </Stack>

        <Stack gap="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)">
          <Text
            variant="label"
            color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
            as="div"
          >
            5-Day Forecast
          </Text>

          <Stack
            direction="horizontal"
            gap="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
            justify="between"
            wrap
            aria-label="5-day forecast"
          >
            {forecast.map((item, index) => (
              <Card
                key={`${item.day ?? 'day'}-${index}`}
                padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
                radius="md"
                shadow="none"
                border
              >
                <Stack align="center" gap="var(--ggui-spacing-1, 4px)">
                  <Text
                    variant="caption"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
                    as="div"
                  >
                    {item.day ?? '—'}
                  </Text>
                  <Text
                    size="xl"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                    as="div"
                    aria-label={`Forecast icon for ${item.day ?? 'day'}`}
                  >
                    {item.icon ?? '—'}
                  </Text>
                  <Text
                    variant="caption"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                    as="div"
                  >
                    {item.high ?? '--'}° / {item.low ?? '--'}°
                  </Text>
                </Stack>
              </Card>
            ))}
          </Stack>
        </Stack>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  );
}

evaluation.critique: The component excellently uses design tokens and renders all data from props without hardcoding. Layout is solid with proper flex usage and responsive wrapping, though the 5-day forecast cards could benefit from better constraint handling on smaller screens (potential overflow). Polish is the weakest area—missing hover/focus states on forecast cards and no loading/empty states for edge cases like empty forecasts.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid overall structure with proper flex/stack usage and responsive scrolling, but has inconsistent design token usage with fallback pixel values and problematic inline styles that override message backgrounds. The data presentation is strong with proper grouping and timestamp formatting, but the visual polish lacks smooth transitions and the inline style approach for message backgrounds is fragile.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

results.error: API key must be provided via constructor or GOOGLE_GENAI_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable.

commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

generation.sourceCode:
import React from 'react';
import {
  Card,
  Stack,
  Text,
  Heading,
  Badge,
  Divider,
  Box,
} from '@ggui-ai/design/primitives';

interface Props {
  /** City name */
  city: string;
  /** Current temperature */
  temperature: number;
  /** Weather condition (Sunny, Cloudy, Rainy, etc.) */
  condition: string;
  /** Humidity percentage */
  humidity: number;
  /** Wind speed */
  windSpeed: number;
  /** Temperature unit (C or F) */
  unit?: string;
  /** 5-day forecast array */
  forecast: Array<{ day?: string; icon?: string; high?: number; low?: number }>;
}

export default function GeneratedComponent(props: Props) {
  const {
    city = 'San Francisco',
    temperature = 72,
    condition = 'Sunny',
    humidity = 65,
    windSpeed = 12,
    unit = 'F',
    forecast = [
      { day: 'Mon', icon: '☀️', high: 75, low: 62 },
      { day: 'Tue', icon: '⛅', high: 73, low: 61 },
      { day: 'Wed', icon: '☁️', high: 68, low: 59 },
      { day: 'Thu', icon: '🌧️', high: 64, low: 55 },
      { day: 'Fri', icon: '☀️', high: 71, low: 60 },
    ],
  } = props;

  // Map condition names to emoji icons
  const conditionIcons: Record<string, string> = {
    Sunny: '☀️',
    Cloudy: '☁️',
    Rainy: '🌧️',
    Snowy: '❄️',
    Stormy: '⛈️',
    'Partly Cloudy': '⛅',
    Foggy: '🌫️',
    Windy: '💨',
    Hail: '🌨️',
  };

  const weatherIcon = conditionIcons[condition] || '🌤️';

  return (
    <Card shadow="md" padding={24} radius="lg">
      <Stack direction="vertical" gap={16}>
        {/* Header: City and Temperature Unit Badge */}
        <Stack direction="horizontal" justify="between" align="center">
          <Heading level={5} color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)">
            {city}
          </Heading>
          {unit && (
            <Badge variant="primary" size="sm">
              °{unit}
            </Badge>
          )}
        </Stack>

        {/* Temperature Display Section */}
        <Stack direction="vertical" gap={8} align="center">
          <Text
            size="4xl"
            weight="bold"
            color="var(--ggui-color-primary-600, #0284c7)"
            align="center"
          >
            {temperature}°
          </Text>
          <Stack direction="horizontal" gap={8} align="center" justify="center">
            <Text size="lg">{weatherIcon}</Text>
            <Text
              variant="body"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
            >
              {condition}
            </Text>
          </Stack>
        </Stack>

        <Divider orientation="horizontal" margin={16} />

        {/* Humidity and Wind Information */}
        <Stack direction="horizontal" justify="between" gap={32}>
          <Stack direction="vertical" gap={4} align="center">
            <Text
              variant="label"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
            >
              Humidity
            </Text>
            <Text
              variant="body"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
              align="center"
            >
              {humidity}%
            </Text>
          </Stack>
          <Stack direction="vertical" gap={4} align="center">
            <Text
              variant="label"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)"
            >
              Wind
            </Text>
            <Text
              variant="body"
              color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
              align="center"
            >
              {windSpeed} mph
            </Text>
          </Stack>
        </Stack>

        <Divider orientation="horizontal" margin={16} />

        {/* 5-Day Forecast Strip */}
        <Stack
          direction="horizontal"
          gap={12}
          wrap
          justify="between"
        >
          {forecast.map((day, index) => (
            <Box
              key={`forecast-${index}`}
              background="var(--ggui-color-primary-50, #f0f9ff)"
              padding={12}
              borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-md, 8px)"
              style={{ flex: '0 0 auto', minWidth: '70px' }}
            >
              <Stack direction="vertical" gap={6} align="center">
                <Text
                  variant="label"
                  color="var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)"
                  align="center"
                  truncate
                >
                  {day.day || 'Day'}
                </Text>
                <Text size="lg" align="center">
                  {day.icon || '—'}
                </Text>
                <Text
                  variant="caption"
                  weight="semibold"
                  color="var(--ggui-color-primary-700, #0369a1)"
                  align="center"
                >
                  {day.high}°
                </Text>
                <Text
                  variant="caption"
                  color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-600, #4b5563)"
                  align="center"
                >
                  {day.low}°
                </Text>
              </Stack>
            </Box>
          ))}
        </Stack>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  );
}

evaluation.critique: The component has solid layout and data handling, but uses some hardcoded fallback colors (e.g., #18181b, #52525b) instead of relying purely on design tokens, and lacks interactive polish like hover states on the forecast cards. The hierarchy is clear with good visual separation via dividers and size contrast.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

evaluation.critique: The component has strong layout and data handling, with proper responsive flexbox usage and all weather data correctly sourced from props. However, there are design token inconsistencies: inline styles with hardcoded font-sizes (72px, 64px, 24px, 20px) bypass the token system, and the gradient header uses fallback hex values instead of pure token references. Interactive polish is good but could be enhanced with hover states on forecast cards.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Perfect, see you both there
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

evaluation.critique: The chat component has a solid layout with proper flex structure, good use of design tokens for colors and spacing, and clear message differentiation. Minor issues: some hardcoded pixel values (16, 4, 6, 8) for Box paddingX/paddingY instead of spacing tokens, and the message bubble border-radius uses raw pixel fallbacks rather than pure token references. The component handles data presentation well with proper timestamp formatting and message grouping, though the disabled state visual feedback could be more prominent.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.
